Who is the traditionally attributed author of the Symposium?

The Symposium (會飲篇) is traditionally attributed to Plato. It is one of his dialogues exploring the nature of love through a series of speeches at a banquet, and is considered part of the Platonic corpus with attributed confidence. Socrates appears as a major character in the dialogue, but authorship belongs to Plato, not Socrates.
